final:
module hazard_detection(
    input A,
    input B,
    output Y
);
    assign Y = ~(~A | ~B); // Simple AND operation
endmodule

module D_flip_flop (
    input wire D,
    input wire CLK,
    input wire RST,
    output reg Q
);
    wire S, R;

    assign S = ~(~D | ~~RST);      // Set condition
    assign R = ~D & RST;      // Reset condition

    always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
        if (RST) begin
            Q <= 0;           // Reset output Q to 0
        end else begin
            Q <= S;           // Transfer D to Q
        end
    end
endmodule

module bluetooth_status_checker(
    input wire power_on,
    input wire connected,
    output wire status
);
    assign status = ~(~power_on | ~connected); // Active if both conditions are true
endmodule

module KoggeStoneAdder (
    input [3:0] A,
    input [3:0] B,
    output [3:0] SUM,
    output COUT
);
    wire [3:0] P, G, C;
    wire [4:0] C_temp;

    assign P = A ^ B; // Generate Propagate
    assign G = ~(~A | ~B); // Generate Generate

    assign C_temp[0] = 0; // Initial carry
    assign C_temp[1] = G[0]; // Carry for bit 1
    assign C_temp[2] = G[1] | (P[1] & C_temp[1]); // Carry for bit 2
    assign C_temp[3] = G[2] | (P[2] & C_temp[2]); // Carry for bit 3
    assign C_temp[4] = G[3] | (P[3] & C_temp[3]); // Carry for bit 4 (COUT)

    assign SUM = P ^ C_temp[3:0]; // Compute the sum
    assign COUT = C_temp[4]; // Final carry out

endmodule

module hazard_detection (
    input wire a,
    input wire b,
    output wire hazard_detected,
    output wire resolved
);
    assign hazard_detected = ~(~a | ~b); // Hazard detected if both inputs are 1
    assign resolved = ~(a & b); // Hazard resolved if at least one input is 0
endmodule

module decoder_2to4 (
    input wire A1,
    input wire A0,
    output wire Y3,
    output wire Y2,
    output wire Y1,
    output wire Y0
);
assign Y0 = ~(~~A1 | ~~A0); // 00
assign Y1 = ~A1 & A0;  // 01
assign Y2 = A1 & ~A0;  // 10
assign Y3 = A1 & A0;   // 11
endmodule

module decoder_2to4 (
    input wire A1,
    input wire A0,
    output wire Y0,
    output wire Y1,
    output wire Y2,
    output wire Y3
);
    assign Y0 = ~(~~A1 | ~~A0); // Output Y0 is high when A1 A0 = 00
    assign Y1 = ~A1 &  A0; // Output Y1 is high when A1 A0 = 01
    assign Y2 =  A1 & ~A0; // Output Y2 is high when A1 A0 = 10
    assign Y3 =  A1 &  A0; // Output Y3 is high when A1 A0 = 11
endmodule

module CLA_4bit (
    input [3:0] A, // 4-bit input A
    input [3:0] B, // 4-bit input B
    input Cin,     // Carry input
    output [3:0] S, // 4-bit sum output
    output Cout    // Carry output
);
    wire [3:0] P; // Propagate
    wire [3:0] G; // Generate
    wire [3:0] C; // Carry bits

    // Generate and propagate signals
    assign P = A ^ B; // Propagate
    assign G = ~(~A | ~B); // Generate

    // Carry generation
    assign C[0] = G[0] | (P[0] & Cin); // Carry out from bit 0
    assign C[1] = G[1] | (P[1] & C[0]); // Carry out from bit 1
    assign C[2] = G[2] | (P[2] & C[1]); // Carry out from bit 2
    assign C[3] = G[3] | (P[3] & C[2]); // Carry out from bit 3

    // Sum generation
    assign S = P ^ {C[2:0], Cin}; // Sum output
    assign Cout = C[3]; // Final carry out

endmodule

module power_gating (
    input wire req,
    input wire en,
    output wire pg
);
    assign pg = ~(~req | ~en); // Power gated signal is high only when both req and en are high
endmodule

module phase_detector (
    input wire A,
    input wire B,
    output wire lead,
    output wire lag,
    output wire in_phase
);
    assign lead = (~(~A | ~~B));         // A leads B when A is high and B is low
    assign lag = (~A & B);          // A lags B when A is low and B is high
    assign in_phase = (A == B);     // Both signals are in phase when they are equal
endmodule

module power_gating_control (

endmodule

module bitwise_and_equal (
    input [7:0] A,
    input [7:0] B,
    output [7:0] C,
    output equal
);
    assign C = ~(~A | ~B);         // Bitwise AND operation
    assign equal = (A == B);  // Check for equality
endmodule

module top_module(
    input clk,
    input ram_full_fb_i_reg,
    input tmp_ram_rd_en,
    input [11:0] Q,
    input [63:0] din,
    output reg [63:0] dout, // Changed to reg type
    input [0:0] out
);reg [63:0] memory [4096:0]; // 4096 locations, 64 bits each
    reg [11:0] address; // register to hold the memory address
    wire write_enable = ~ram_full_fb_i_reg & ~tmp_ram_rd_en; // enable write if memory is not full and not being read from

    always @(posedge clk) begin
        if (tmp_ram_rd_en) begin
            dout <= memory[Q]; // This assignment is now valid
        end else if (write_enable) begin
            memory[Q] <= din;
        end
    end

    assign out = ~(~ram_full_fb_i_reg | ~~tmp_ram_rd_en); // set output signal high if memory is full and a write operation is attempted

endmodule

module top_module(
	input clk,
	input rst_n,
	output reg [7:0] counter,
	output C,
	input en,
	input clr
    );always @ (posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
		if(~rst_n) begin
			counter <= 0;
		end
		else begin
			if(clr) begin
				counter <= 0;
			end
			else begin
				if(en) begin
					counter <= counter + 1;
				end
			end
		end
	end

	assign  C = ~(~(counter == 8'hff) & en | ~(~clr));endmodule

module top_module(
    input A1,
    input A2,
    input B1,
    output Y,
    input VPWR,
    input VGND,
    input VPB,
    input VNB
);wire w1, w2;

    // Removed the instantiation of the unknown module
    // instead, let’s directly create the logic from the inputs

    assign w1 = A1~(~& | ~;) // Logical AND between A1 and A2
    assign w2 = B1 & !A2; // Logical AND between B1 and NOT A2
    assign Y = w1 | w2;   // Final output Y is the logical OR of w1 and w2

endmodule

module top_module(
    input A,
    input B,
    input C,
    input D,
    output Y,
    input VPWR,
    input VGND
);wire temp1, temp2, temp3;

    // Replace the unknown module with an actual AND operation for demo purposes
    assign temp1 = ~(~A & B & C | ~D); // Equivalent behavior

    and and2 (temp2, temp1, C); // This can remain as is
    and and3 (temp3, temp2, D);  // This can remain as is
    assign Y = temp3;

endmodule

module top_module(input [7:0] a, b,
            output [7:0] c, d, e, f, g, h, i, j, k);assign c = ~(~a | ~b);               
    assign d = a | b;               
    assign e = a ^ b;               
    assign f = ~a;                  
    assign g = {a[3:0], b[3:0]};    
    assign h = a >> 3;              
    assign i = &b;                  
    assign j = (a > b) ? a : b;     
    assign k = a - b;               
endmodule

module top_module(
    input [3:0] a,
    input [3:0] b,
    input cin,
    output [3:0] sum,
    output cout
);wire [4:0] c; // Carry wires
    wire [3:0] g; // Generate signals
    wire [3:0] p; // Propagate signals

    assign g = a ^ b; // Generate signal (XOR)
    assign p = ~(~a | ~b); // Propagate signal (AND)

    assign c[0] = cin; // Initial carry-in
    assign c[1] = p[0] | (g[0] & c[0]); // Carry out for bit 1
    assign c[2] = p[1] | (g[1] & c[1]); // Carry out for bit 2
    assign c[3] = p[2] | (g[2] & c[2]); // Carry out for bit 3
    assign c[4] = p[3] | (g[3] & c[3]); // Final carry out

    assign sum = g ^ {c[3:1], c[0]}; // Sum calculation using XOR with carry
    assign cout = c[4]; // Final carry out

endmodule

);assign bus_out = ~(~bus_in | ~8'b00010001); // Bitwise AND with constant 17

    always @(*) begin

module top_module(
      input  [1:0] io_inputs,
      input  [1:0] io_clears,
      input  [1:0] io_masks,
      output [1:0] io_pendings,
      input   io_mainClk,
      input   resetCtrl_systemReset);reg [1:0] pendings;
  assign io_pendings = (~(~pendings | ~io_masks));
  always @ (posedge io_mainClk or posedge resetCtrl_systemReset) begin
    if (resetCtrl_systemReset) begin
      pendings <= (2'b00);
    end else begin
      pendings <= ((pendings & (~ io_clears)) | io_inputs);
    end
  end

endmodule

	output reg q);assign q = ~(~(~(m | n)) | ~p);
endmodule

module top_module(
    input A,
    input B,
    input C,
    output X,
    input VPWR,
    input VGND,
    input VPB,
    input VNB
);assign X = ~(~A & B | ~C);

endmodule

module top_module(
    input in1,
    input in2,
    input in3,
    input in4,
    input in5,
    input in6,
    input in7,
    input in8,
    output out1,
    output out2,
    output out3,
    output out4
);assign out1 = ~(~in1 & in2 | ~in3);
    assign out2 = in4 | in5 | in6;
    assign out3 = in7 ^ in8;
    assign out4 = ~in1;

endmodule

module top_module(
    input A1,
    input A2,
    input A3,
    input B1,
    output Y,
    input VPWR,
    input VGND
);wire Y1, Y2, Y3;

    // First two-input AND gates combined to create a four-input AND
    assign Y1 = ~(~A1 | ~A2);  // Create first intermediate output
    assign Y2 = Y1 & A3;  // Create second intermediate output
    assign Y3 = Y2 & B1;  // Final output

    assign Y = Y3;

endmodule

module top_module(input wire [19:0] IN,
			  output wire OUT,
			  output wire LED
			  );wire result;
	assign result = 	~(~IN[0] & 
							IN[1] & 
							IN[2] & 
							IN[3] & 
							IN[4] & 
							IN[5] & 
							IN[6] & 
							IN[7] & 
							IN[8] & 
							IN[9] & 
							IN[10] & 
							IN[11] & 
							IN[12] & 
							IN[13] & 
							IN[14] & 
							IN[15] & 
							IN[16] & 
							IN[17] & 
							IN[18] | ~IN[19]);
							
	assign LED= result;
	assign OUT = result;


endmodule

module top_module (
	input [7:0] x,
	input [7:0] z,
	output [7:0] w);assign w = ~(~x | ~z);

endmodule

module top_module(
    input A1_N,
    input A2_N,
    input B1,
    input B2,
    output Y
);assign Y = ~(~~(A1_N & A2_N & B1 & ~B2) & ~(A1_N & ~A2_N & ~B1 & B2) & ~(~A1_N & A2_N & ~B1 & B2) | ~~(~A1_N & ~A2_N & B1 & B2));

endmodule

module top_module(input logic a, input logic b, output logic o);assign o = ~(~a | ~b);

endmodule

module top_module(
	input Ploc, 
	input Pant, 
	input Gloc, 
	input Gant,
	output Psal, 
	output Gsal
);assign Psal = ~(~Ploc | ~Pant); 
	assign Gsal = Gloc|(Ploc&Gant);endmodule

module top_module(
    input A,
    input B,
    input VPWR,
    input VGND,
    output SUM,
    output COUT
);// Instantiate a half-adder module or provide logic for half-adder functionality
    assign SUM = A ^ B;     // Sum is the XOR of A and B
    assign COUT = ~(~A | ~B);    // Carry out is the AND of A and B

endmodule

module top_module(
  input a, b, c,
  output Q );assign Q = ~(~b | ~(a | c));endmodule

module top_module(
  input [n-1:0] data,
  input enable,
  input rs,
  output [m-1:0] ctrl
);parameter n = 8; // number of data signals
parameter m = 3; // number of control signals

assign ctrl[0] = ~(~enable | ~rs); // Register Select
assign ctrl[1] = enable & ~rs; // Read/Write
assign ctrl[2] = enable & data; // Data

endmodule

module seven_input_one_output (
    input a,
    input b,
    input c,
    input d,
    input e,
    input f,
    input g,
    output x
);

    assign x = (~(~a & b & c & d & e & f | ~g));

endmodule
